scenarios.title: Protein Structure Prediction and Design Workflows
scenarios.when_to_read: AlphaFold-style biomolecular structure prediction, Protenix CLI use, input JSON preparation, MSA/template/RNA preprocessing, Protenix training data, or Protenix kernel/config troubleshooting.
scenarios.role: Use `protenix` for Protenix-specific structure prediction, preprocessing, training-data, and model/backend workflows.
scenarios.read_when: User mentions Protenix, protenix pred/json/msa/mt/prep, AlphaFold 3-style JSON, proteins with DNA/RNA/ligands/ions, MSA/template/RNA MSA, PROTENIX_ROOT_DIR, Protenix checkpoints, cuEquivariance/Triton/DeepSpeed kernels, TFG, Protenix confidence metrics, or Protenix training/fine-tuning.
scenarios.best_for: Building safe Protenix commands, validating Protenix input JSON, checking MSA/template/RNA layouts, planning custom CIF preprocessing, preparing training or fine-tuning launches, and diagnosing Protenix-specific install/backend/config failures.
scenarios.avoid_when: Use a different protein skill when the user names another package such as Boltz, Chai-1, ESM, ProteinMPNN, RFdiffusion, or ColabFold and Protenix is not involved; use generic PyTorch or MLOps skills for non-Protenix training infrastructure.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Choose Protenix when the named repository/package, CLI command, JSON schema, data-root layout, or kernel/config issue is Protenix-specific. Among overlapping structure-prediction skills, Protenix is the best fit for AlphaFold 3-style multimolecule JSON plus Protenix's own pred/json/msa/mt/prep CLI and training-data pipeline.
